accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text or are verified accurate context (e.g., FTC enforcement, Youville as an FTC program).

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; contextual additions like Youville being an FTC program are accurate.

states_purpose.why: The summary clearly identifies the bill's purpose of protecting minors online via requirements on platforms, games, and chatbots.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Bill amends the Protecting Children in the 21st Century Act and references COPPA/FTC Act; summary mentions FTC enforcement and education campaigns but only lightly touches the specific statutory amendment, though it adequately captures the operative effect.

exceptions_conditions.why: Summary notes key carve-outs like exceptions for safety/legal compliance in research, no government ID requirement, and the knowledge-of-minor standard.

effective_dates.why: Summary correctly notes the general one-year effective date with some provisions taking effect earlier (e.g., 180-day and 90-day subtitles).

major_provisions_covered.why: All six titles' essential operative effects are captured accurately and at appropriate high level.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary is well-organized by title and reads clearly and consistently.

concise.why: The summary is appropriately compact without padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: FTC, state attorneys general, and the Kids Internet Safety Partnership are correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: Stated figures (under 17 for minor, under 13 for children, one-third sexual material threshold, three-hour break, one-year effective date) all match the bill.
